title: Risk Management System
summary: High-risk AI providers must establish, implement, document, and maintain a continuous risk management system across the AI lifecycle.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement should cover systematic risk identification, analysis, mitigation, residual-risk evaluation, and monitoring after deployment. For a healthcare AI system, it supports requirements for documented risk controls, review cycles, and post-market feedback loops.

title: Data Governance
summary: Training, validation, and testing datasets for high-risk AI must meet quality criteria, including representativeness and bias examination.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement concerns dataset quality, provenance, preparation, relevance, representativeness, error detection, bias examination, and appropriate data governance practices. In healthcare AI, it is especially relevant to patient cohorts, demographic coverage, labelling quality, missing data, and privacy-aware data handling.

title: Record-keeping (Logging)
summary: High-risk AI systems must automatically log events to ensure traceability of operation and post-market monitoring.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement needs audit trails, event logging, traceability, monitoring evidence, or reviewable operational records. For clinical systems, it supports recording access, model output events, human overrides, timestamps, and system state needed for investigation.

title: Transparency and Information to Users
summary: Users must be provided with clear, intelligible instructions on AI capabilities, limitations, and intended purpose.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement should describe intended use, operating conditions, known limitations, expected accuracy, user instructions, and interpretation guidance. For healthcare professionals, it supports clear communication of what the AI can and cannot safely support.

title: Human Oversight
summary: High-risk AI must allow effective human oversight to prevent or minimise risks to health, safety, or fundamental rights.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement needs human review, override, escalation, monitoring, or safe intervention. In clinical workflows, it supports keeping clinicians in control of consequential decisions and making AI recommendations contestable.

title: Accuracy, Robustness and Cybersecurity
summary: High-risk AI must reach an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity throughout its lifecycle.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement covers accuracy targets, robustness testing, resilience to errors, cybersecurity controls, degradation handling, or ongoing performance monitoring. For healthcare AI, it supports validation under realistic clinical conditions and safeguards against unsafe model drift.
